title: The string-built query
summary: The AI wired up a DB lookup from user input. Anything jump out?
task: Look up a user by the username from the request.

hiddenIssues.title: SQL injection via string interpolation
hiddenIssues.explanation: `username` is interpolated straight into the SQL, so an input like `' OR '1'='1` returns the whole table. Happy-path tests pass, types pass, and most linters stay quiet. A security review (or a SAST scan / raw-SQL lint rule) catches it. Fix: a parameterized query — db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?', [username]).

debrief: The dangerous ones don't fail tests or types — the happy path passes and the types are fine. Only thinking about security (a review, or a scanner for raw SQL) catches injection. AI loves building queries by string concatenation; this is the one to watch for.
